GN[19x19 ladder challenge: takkaa vs Uberdude]
takkaa: Yes, I have seen it few times.

C[Uberdude: This is the first move I have doubts about. This is common with black at r14, less so with q14. I could just answer this with the simple o18 p17 k17 l16 j17 r17 r16 p18 which was my original plan with f17. But then I decided q14 makes the o16 cut more lively. Also extension to l17 is usually a bit heavy, but with f17 support it should be ok. So I think instead of this which probably helps black more than white I would just o18 (then m16 r17 miai, can't remember which I planned to take) or r17 directly (then o18 and white can p17 or dodge to s15 after which black p18 and white comes up at r13 which looks nice to trash black's side there and weakens r9).
C[Uberdude: First time I think I've played this. Looks better than q17.

C[Uberdude: I think saving these now is your first big mistake. o18 better: I will answer with thick l16 (because after o18 saving these stones is much more severe as when you play l16 I can't hane or you cut). That would mean you got a big corner in sente and can play a big opening move like d18 or something on the lower side. You also have q10 reduction.
C[Uberdude: This move is so very painful for white. If you didn't play n14 (but d18 instead for example) and then I played here you can tenuki, but now you have to connect or n14 is stupid.
C[Uberdude: o18 over 10 points in sente, and s16 is also my sente now.
C[Uberdude: Black is happy to make lots of points and f17 strong.

C[Uberdude: It's good shape, but the move that would annoy me would be e17: if I answer at f16 it's overconcentrated and you strengthen your corner in sente. But tenuki hurts too. W group is cutting 2 strong black groups.
C[Uberdude: Avoids e17 annoying move and takes your base.

C[Uberdude: This move is nearly always wrong. Should d17, then black d18 and then you can switch to this direction if you want (instead of e18) so b18 b17 c18 c11. Then black corner is smaller and w doesn't fear e16. Sometimes black can answer d17 with strong c16 push to take more white eyespace in corner but as you have d15 ladder that's nothing to fear.
C[Uberdude: This does lose me n2 aji, but decided to bulk up this group. If I don't play here white r8 looked annoying.
C[Uberdude: Surprising tenuki, but it looks good.

C[Uberdude: This connection is weak. I can only resist s12 thanks to s12 r11 r12 p11. So if you play p9, which I want to answer at p6, then s12 works. I was planning to p6 anyway and fight, but it's complex.
C[Uberdude: Big move. You could also play something like d7 or e8.
C[Uberdude: Wow, immediate fight. Seems black should do better locally. Maybe white can use the p9 stuff to get o13 running out and get them to a splitting attack in the centre.
C[Uberdude: I wanted to h4 or j4, but this group seemed to need more help.
C[Uberdude: Seems a bad exchange, strengthens black and doesn't 100% fix the f5 weakness.
C[Uberdude: Run at k4 and then k7 was my plan. f5 too small for now, but rather annoying w can fix in sente with e7 and d6.

GN[19x19 ladder challenge: MarkGoldfain vs takkaa]
C[takkaa: Greetings from Warsaw, Poland. gg
C[takkaa: Greetings from Warsaw, Poland. gg
C[takkaa: This move seems bad. It is much too early to play this invasion. White is happy to trade corner territory for a wall that will combine nicely with a pincer against q5 stone.
C[MarkGoldfain: I expected P17 here, instead of P16.  Is there a reason behind it?

takkaa: Playing p17 joseki would mean that White ends in gote. Black would be happy to play on the right side erasing White's influence and supporting his q5 stone. I think p17 would be playable if White already had a stone around q9.
C[takkaa: This is not joseki. Crawling on the third line seems slow and White is happy with his wall. Standard s16 seems better.
C[MarkGoldfain: As far as I can tell, this move was going to cost me the game.  Perhaps Black has a good response, though I did not find it.
takkaa: White presses twice, m17 would be good shape but right side is more important. Now White can hope to make lots of points on the right side.
C[takkaa: I would prefer solid o5 instead. Having strong group here would make sense if Black aims at invading/reducing White's sphere of influence above.
C[takkaa: I have never played it before so I tried this fighting move. I am not sure if it is the best decision but other moves seemed to strengthen Black which I would like to avoid.
C[takkaa: It is nothing bad, it is a common practice here.

C[MarkGoldfain: I had followed the joseki up to this point, but I felt it works out badly for Black in the overall board position, because White will get to press hard on the 4 Black stones trapped at the right edge, and if they make life, White will probably get another wall and have totally secured a huge number of points at the upper right side.
I was hoping Black could get free from this, even if it gave more points than it should to the White group at the bottom.  I was hoping Black could find some way to press upward.
takkaa: I have seen this move in dan games so I guess it is possible.
C[takkaa: I was happy to see this move, q7 seemed much more difficult to handle due to d17 ladder breaker.
C[takkaa: White manages to stop Black but his moyo is somewhat open with bad aji around r11. I think up to now Black is ok.
C[MarkGoldfain: If this goes about the same as the lower right, it won't result in the same kind of point gain for Black, I assume.  But it stil
column2: l might balance the board some, to counteract

column1: White's expansion from the right into the center.  (This is the thinking of a 7kyu, so it may have no basis in reality :-)
takkaa: I agree with your judgement. I think there are few possibilities here, but this move seems ok. It combines nicely with d4 and m3.
C[takkaa: I decide to play solidly.
C[takkaa: It connects nicely with other White's stones. Black's invasion seems inevitable now.
C[MarkGoldfain: Okay, I have to try something.  Any advice regarding a move such as this?
takkaa: Seems a bit too deep but I do not see good way to attack so maybe it is ok.
C[takkaa: It is hard to directly attack n12. Playing k3 aims at provoking Black's group to run to the middle. Next White hopes for splitting attack against n12 stone and n3 group.
C[takkaa: White eliminates the aji on the right and gets a footing on the bottom but Black is ok I think, l5 seems to be very well placed.
C[takkaa: I do not like this move. It does not create any territory, it does not attack White, it does not support n12. Anything in the centre would be better I think.
C[takkaa: White gets the opportunity to attack as he planned.
C[takkaa: The situation is tough for Black now. White's group on the bottom is safe so he can attack.
C[takkaa: White destroys Black's shape. If Black connects White continues to attack from h7 direction possibly destroying left side. n12 stone is really in troubles.
C[MarkGoldfain: I can't say how this fight has gone so far.  Perhaps Black has not embarrassed himself yet.

takkaa: This fight went bad for Black, he has not really achieved anything. His group still has no eyes, can be cut off at k6. White's attacking group is rock-solid now and White secured some point in the middle.
C[takkaa: The ladder does not work and Black lost the game. Of course Black can aim at r11 cut but White has p10 so he does not have to worry.

C[MarkGoldfain: This movef, O13, is evidently a mis-read.  Perhaps settling for P12 is better.  But I thought I could press a little farther, and it might spell lots more trouble for the White Q10 group.  If Black could kill that, I think it would be a game-changer, because I don't think White would get enough compensation.
takkaa: If White two stones cannot be caught the game is over. Even after P12 White has lots of point in the middle, Black's group is still not safe and left side can be easily invaded.
C[MarkGoldfain: I can finally read it out differently at this point.  So, Black's stones above this at Q11 were all an overplay.  Perhaps the game is over at this point.
takkaa: White is safe and aims at k6 now.

C[MarkGoldfain: Is this actually valid?  I thought that White R11 would keep these stones from escaping.  So, White's move at O9 surprised me.  Or is it that Black needs then to worry about its larger group to the left instead?
takkaa: Perhaps R11 is better but o9 solves all White's problems now.

C[MarkGoldfain: It looks like this just killed 13 black stones, and it leaves a wave of white stones flowing up to the left.  Unless there is some tesuji for Black that I never saw, I gather that the game was over at this point, if it had not already been a sure win for White even before this point.
takkaa: I do not think that Black can live. Even if he can White's middle territory is very big and Black cannot hope on many points on the left.
takkaa: This is the last comment.

C[Celebrir: I think the opening is okay and normal for both sides up to here.
takkaa: I agree, I don't see any bad move up to now.

C[Celebrir: I'm not sure whether this is great because it looks really small now. Otherwise it would hurt if you get to play there... Maybe I should attack your left with a move P14 to reinforce the top.

takkaa: Yes, it seems slow but I think it is ok. Probably I would play here if you did not and I think it would be troublesome for big keima shimari. It is gote but reinforces loose shimari so ok in my opinion.
C[Celebrir: I think this is bad and to soon. I'm not sure whether I did it correctly but it should be possible to start a double attack at this stone and the right side.

takkaa: I know it is bad :-) But since I have not much time to think back then I played it. I like to play more dynamic openings and I wanted to create a bit of action. I am not afraid of double attack - right-side group can always play r8 which seems to be sente against the corner. But undoubtedly the move is bad :-)
takkaa: Anyway, I am not sure what to play now. What about going for moyo with G5 followed by F15 cap? Seems giving to much points for quite shallow moyo.
Celebrir: I guess that would be exactly what I play because that B has to invade and W coudl strength to do some good attacks. Maybe I would G15 instead of F15 to prevent a B move at G15 after F15

C[takkaa: I do not like it. It gives forcing moves to White and leaves aji in the corner. I would play p17 here. White would have to jump and Black would reinforce the top group still keeping the pressure.
C[Celebrir: Maybe this is to passive and L15 should be played here.
C[Celebrir: Maybe first leaning with Q12 followed up by something like M14 is better ?
Celebrir: This comment should have been at move 39 -.-
C[Celebrir: I think the topic should be still the top right.
takkaa: Right, very greedy. M14 seems to solve White's problems for a while.
C[takkaa: Yes, I like q12 here instead of n13. I would have to respond and then Black's attack on a larger scale would be very troublesome for White.
C[takkaa: I think this cut was very nice for White. I feel I did not use it to the full advantage. I think Black should have protected with O13.

Celebrir: That seemed to slow to me. I didn't fear the cut because I was relativly sure to either get something at the right or run away easily with this group. If I had protected you could easily escape with your group and that would really hurt.

takkaa: Still I think Black should play Q13 without hesitation - it is sente and makes his position rock-solid. Black could continue the attack on a large scale with sth like J9 making lots of points at the bottom.
Celebrir: Q13 Doesn't seem that much like sente to me. W can sacriefiece a few stones for the benefit of saving a large group and securing the right in the case B takes the stones.
takkaa: After White ignores Q13 and plays in the middle there is no right to secure anymore and White has lost on points.
C[Celebrir: Was there a hidden plan here or did you misread ? Can't make sense out of this move
takkaa: It seems to protect both cuts after Black plays L11.
Celebrir: I thought J12 for W would be enough to safe this group after L11. I think O11 is to much to give me here.
takkaa: I do not think Black wins much here. His group is already safe, my both groups are weak and Black can escape to the bottom. O11 peep gives nothing to White.
Celebrir: I think it does something. Look at the final position: I think W could live if the cut would be still there.
C[takkaa: Black cannot push with L11 now as White would respond with L12 atari.
Celebrir: Sure, but N11 fixed my problem as well and make the left weaker which I was aiming at the whole time
C[Celebrir: You should reinforce the middle one more in my opinion. Sure, I would get some attack at the left but that should be the punishment for such a weak group
takkaa: Right but I need points. Left is what I have. I tried to come up with a move that expands the left and helps to the middle group.
Celebrir: Maybe H9 is better because it works together with the wall at the left bottom ?
C[Celebrir: Note that O7 still makes a false eye so the middle is not alive completly.

takkaa: Of course but I need to support the right. Middle group should live I think. The only way to kill it by Black is to play directly O6 which would give to White lots of forcing moves against O6 and afterwards N3 etc.

Celebrir: True I need some extra stones around there to kill the middle but I think it would be enough for me to catch K3. Therefore I have some forcing move there as well which could be enough to attack the middle. I didn't read it out because I thought the right side is more promising and if you save it, it should be sente for me so that I can still think about the bottom later.
C[Celebrir: Nice move, I missed this. However, it doesen't makes live in sente I think
C[takkaa: Yes, this is one tennuki too much. I did not check if the right can leave, just took points.
C[takkaa: And it is over I think.
Celebrir: I agree. I thought quite a while about this but didn't found a way to life for W.
C[Celebrir: So finally I got something for your weak group. However I think my attack wasn't great as well and I only was able to get something because you tennukied 3 times to soon in my opinion.
takkaa: Bad invasion made it difficult for White. But as I do not have much time for go at the moment I prefer to lose dynamic game than win peaceful one :-)
Celebrir: Yeah I know that feeling of not having as much time as I want to play *sighs* Especially here where you could think about so much possibilities...

C[takkaa: This was the first problematic move for me. What would you suggest?

chiahsun: (1) If you take E3(claim the corner) then I have to take F4(to add force on J4 and avoid white to put on F4 since it's big and has great influence for the left side) just as we did in the game.

(2) You may take C6(be conservative to support left side and take J4 as a light move since it's on the forth line) and I will take O17(claim the right-up corner).  Then either F16 or Q5 would be the next move. (3) White to put on K5 might be another interesting move. (4) White to put on J2 would be the most aggressive move.
C[takkaa: This White's move seems so passive now.

chiahsun: Conversely, I think this is a good and active move for White. It avoids Black to take J2 to connect the groups. On the other side, if White takes D8 (a defensive move)instead, then Black cannot take J2(connect the groups but have no force on J2) directly. Black may put R9 to form a big picture and seek time to put J2.
C[takkaa: White's shape is bad. Maybe it was better to play lightly like L5?
chiahsun: Since I give up the Black stones(B12, C12), I have some rights around D12.

(1) If White take L5 then Black would put N4 and take Black(F5, F4, F3) lightly. But if White put K7 in the following move, I think it is still hard for Black to decide whether to broaden right side and focus on the middle stones.

(2) White can reserve the rights to put G6 and continue the move(Your L5, for example). Once the weakness on J3 is solved, White can put on F6 directly and for the best situation is that there is no possibility for Black to revive on the left-down corner and Black(F3,4,5) can be eaten clearly.
C[takkaa: This was a huge mistake I think. Killing K6 instead seems to be better. Right?
chiahsun: It would be clear to put K6 to connect the groups for White. Then, Black put on M6. White has to put K6. Then, I would place J17.

C[takkaa: It seemed to me that whole time in this battle Black is one step ahead. Black got a lot on the outside. Weak M8 group means cash for Black on the right. I think White cannot really do anything about it so White's decides to play the cut which seemed to bring quite a lot of points for White.
chiahsun: Yes, I should have to put O8 for Black, not F2.
C[takkaa: Black's influence in the middle starts to be enormous so I decided to use sente to strengthen M8 group.
C[takkaa: I have to invade once again. Too many weak groups to handle.
C[takkaa: I was quite satisfied here knowing that I can live here and reduce the middle.
C[takkaa: This one is nice. I completely overlooked this possibility. I do not think White can save both, right?
chiahsun: Possibly White can survive for both parts if G17 is taken previously.
C[takkaa: Now it seems the only hope for White is to reduce Black as much as possible.
C[takkaa: Here it seemed to me that if White lived at the top the game would be close (assuming that C3 is dead)...
chiahsun: Yes, if C3 is assume dead, White has an almost 20 stones advantage.
C[takkaa: And now the most important moment. Can White do anything?
chiahsun: (1) Let Black survives only the B5 groups.
Black is not possible to connect F2 groups.
B(B5) -> W(B6) -> B(D2) -> W(E2) -> B(E1) -> W(C2) -> B(D1) -> W(B2) -> B(B3) -> W(A2) (Black B5 groups dies and F2 groups only one eye if taking G2)
So, if Black survives, White is not enough.
(2) But for the least, Black can survive.
Black can survive anyway, in my opinion.

C[takkaa: I think that H3 started White's problems. As a result of the fight on the bottom Black got a lot of thickness. White's efforts to reduce seemed rather desperate - something had to die. And in the end dead corner turns to be alive...
takkaa: Once again thanks for the game.
chiahsun: Thanks for the game and your review comments!
